title: Foster Support for Vaccination in Your Practice | CDC
text:
and parents about upcoming vaccination appointments and missed appointments 
Ongoing communication is essential in making sure patients stay on schedule with vaccinations.
Make calls, send texts, send emails, or mail postcards to remind patients and parents about the importance of vaccination and note upcoming appointments. 
If a patient or parent comes in for a sick visit, remind them about upcoming vaccines. 
If a patient misses an appointment, call to follow up and remind them about the importance of vaccines. 
Resources for Educating Patients Get resources for educating patients and parents about the importance of on-time vaccination.
#HowIRecommend Video Series Get tips from your peers on how to make effective vaccine recommendations and answer common questions.
Top of Page 
Last Reviewed: November 2, 2020 Source: National Center for Immunization and Respiratory Diseases
